_meta.name: viseme_map
_meta.description: Numbered Preston-Blair viseme set. IDs are NUMBERS 1-10 (NOT letters) so a pose id can never collide with a phoneme letter. The numbers match the Blair pose grid (working/blair_pose_grid.png) used to brief the image generator. Single source of truth shared by: (1) timing layer (poc2_align.py) which emits a timeline of {t, viseme_id}; (2) pose layer which provides one sprite PER id; (3) integration layer (PixiJS player) which swaps sprites on the timeline.
_meta.notes: 10 numbered shapes from the Blair grid. id 7 (W/Q) folds into id 6 (U/pucker) so only 9 distinct SPRITES are rendered (rendered_set); 7 reuses 6's sprite. Sprite files are mouth_01.png .. mouth_10.png (zero-padded to sort correctly). id 10 = closed/rest mouth (silence, default between words). NO LETTER IDS ANYWHERE - filenames like pose_A in working/ mean the SOUND 'A/ah', which maps to id 4.

1.label: closed - M/B/P
1.desc: Lips pressed together. Bilabials: m, b, p.

2.label: consonants
2.desc: Slightly open, teeth close. c, d, g, k, n, r, s, t, th, y, z.
2.sound: C D G K N R S Th Y Z

3.label: open - E/eh
3.desc: Open mouth. Vowel eh (bet), ae (bat), the 'E' sound.

4.label: wide open - A/ah
4.desc: Wide open. Vowels aa (father), ah, the 'A/I' sound.

7.label: rounded fwd - W/Q
7.desc: Rounded forward. w, q. Folds into id 6 (shares its sprite).

8.label: teeth on lip - F/V
8.desc: Upper teeth on lower lip. Labiodentals f, v.

9.label: tongue - L
9.desc: Tongue raised/visible. l, and th when stylized.

10.label: rest / idle
10.desc: Mouth closed at rest. Silence, pauses, default between words.

phoneme_to_viseme._comment: ARPABET phoneme -> numbered viseme id. faster-whisper gives WORDS not phonemes, so poc2 first maps via the grapheme heuristic below; this table is the upgrade target when/if a phone-level aligner (MFA) is wired in. Stress digits (0/1/2) are stripped before lookup.

grapheme_to_viseme._comment: Fallback used by poc2 since faster-whisper emits orthographic words, not phonemes. Per-letter heuristic; vowels dominate openness. Crude but enough to validate the pipeline. Lowercased letter -> numbered viseme id.
